sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Jacqueline Miller presented with a history of perioral dermatitis, rosacea, and ocular rosacea.
She reports that her condition has been improving with doxycycline, but has plateaued since switching from twice a day to once a day dosing.
She also mentions a small residual rash on her face.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: Not mentioned.
Current medications:
Doxycycline 1 tablet once a day for acne.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient is Fitzpatrick skin type IV, with a few faint erythematous papules on bilateral medial cheeks and redness around and underneath the nostrils.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has perioral dermatitis, rosacea, and ocular rosacea.
Problem list: Perioral dermatitis; rosacea; ocular rosacea; status: active.
Plan:
I instructed the patient to continue using the sulfacetamide wash once a day, the metrocream after washing her face, and to increase the doxycycline to twice a day.
I also recommended the patient pick up the tacrolimus ointment and start using it on the rash area twice a day until the rash resolves.
After the rash is gone, she will stop the tacrolimus ointment and continue the doxycycline for another week before stopping.
She will also continue to use the sulfacetamide wash and metrocream.
I will have her return in 4 weeks for a follow-up.
Follow-up: I scheduled a follow-up appointment in 4 weeks to reassess the patient's condition.
